LSU AgCenter · Rice Breeding · Molecular Genetics

Developing rice varieties through genetics, data, and field-tested breeding.

Plant breeder and molecular geneticist with 8+ years leading applied medium-grain rice breeding, molecular marker deployment, genomic selection, breeding pipeline management, stakeholder engagement, and cultivar development for Louisiana and the Southern U.S. rice industry.

Explore researchDownload CVView publications
Current role: Associate Professor, LSU AgCenter
Base: H. Rouse Caffey Rice Research Station, Rayne, Louisiana
Focus: Rice breeding, molecular genetics, genomic selection, cultivar development

About

Faculty profile

A public-facing profile built from the CV, refined for collaborators, producers, students, and industry partners.

Professional summary

My research program integrates field breeding, molecular genetics, marker-assisted selection, genomic selection, and applied breeding data science. I focus on developing commercially relevant rice varieties and improving selection efficiency through molecular tools and robust breeding pipelines.

Program philosophy

The breeding program is stakeholder-driven: field days, grower meetings, producer collaborations, trial-data dissemination, and industry partnerships help align research priorities with the needs of Louisiana and Southern U.S. rice production.

Research program

Research highlights

Core themes that define the rice breeding and molecular genetics program.

1

Rice breeding & cultivar development

Parent selection, population development, field testing, greenhouse and winter nursery advancement, seed purification, and commercial line development.

2

Molecular breeding lab leadership

High-throughput KASP/PACE genotyping, seed purity testing, trait introgression support, marker validation, assay development, and laboratory operations.

3

Genomic selection & analytics

R-based breeding data analysis, statistical modeling, QTL analysis, genomic prediction, Shiny applications, and decision-support tools for breeding teams.

Genomic selectionMarker-assisted selectionKASP/PACEQTL analysisRice quality traitsWinter nurseryR & ShinyStakeholder outreach

Academic leadership

Experience

Academic, research, teaching, and industry appointments from the CV.

Aug 2025 - Present

Associate Professor, tenure track

H. Rouse Caffey Rice Research Station, Louisiana State University AgCenter
  • Lead applied medium-grain rice breeding and commercial variety development.
  • Guide breeding decisions using field, greenhouse, winter nursery, marker-assisted, and genomic selection strategies.
Jul 2024 - Jul 2025

Associate Professor Research

H. Rouse Caffey Rice Research Station, Louisiana State University AgCenter
  • Continued leadership of rice breeding and molecular breeding initiatives with expanded research responsibilities.
Dec 2018 - Jun 2024

Assistant Professor Research

H. Rouse Caffey Rice Research Station, Louisiana State University AgCenter
  • Led a medium-grain rice breeding program from parent selection to seed purification and commercial line advancement.
  • Managed a high-throughput molecular genotyping laboratory supporting long, medium, and specialty grain rice programs.
  • Integrated marker-assisted and genomic selection to improve breeding efficiency and genetic gain.
  • Coordinated field, greenhouse, and winter nursery operations and collaborated with multidisciplinary teams.
Feb 2017 - Nov 2018

Research Associate Specialist

H. Rouse Caffey Rice Research Station, Louisiana State University AgCenter
  • Managed marker laboratory operations for marker-assisted selection, seed purity testing, and trait introgression.
  • Developed and validated genome-wide and trait-specific markers for Louisiana-adapted rice germplasm.
2010 - 2016

Graduate Teaching & Research Assistant

Texas A&M University, Department of Soil and Crop Science
  • Taught Crop Stress Management and conducted cowpea breeding, SNP mapping, and QTL research for heat tolerance and agronomic traits.
2008 - 2010

Graduate Research Assistant

West Texas A&M University, Dryland Institute
  • Investigated transpiration efficiency in corn under varying planting geometries.
2007 - 2008

Market Development Officer

United Phosphorus Ltd, Indore, India
  • Supported sales, customer relationship management, product promotion, and client partnerships.

Selected grants

Selected funded projects and major collaborations from the CV.
